# UC-22.2.49 · NIS2 Art.23 — Significant Incident Reporting Package Completeness and Timeline Control

## Description

For every ES notable classified as NIS2-significant, expands the required reporting-stage checklist (early-warning, notification, intermediate updates, final report, cross-border assessment, recipient notice, public-disclosure assessment, template validation) and identifies stages where the corresponding evidence artefact in `index=audit_evidence` has not yet been written — surfacing both upcoming stages and overdue ones.

## Value

Art.23 imposes a multi-stage reporting lifecycle per significant incident. Individual UCs (22.2.1, 22.2.7, 22.2.8) monitor specific clocks, but no single UC confirms the *entire* package is complete. This roll-up UC gives the Head of IR a per-incident checklist view so that a missing stage (e.g. the recipient notice or the anonymised data contribution) is caught before the auditor asks for it.

## Implementation

(1) Build `nis2_reporting_stages.csv` defining each stage, its deadline, and whether it's required or conditional. (2) Ensure each upstream UC writes an artefact with a `stage` field and `parent_event_id` to `index=audit_evidence`. (3) Schedule every 4 hours over 90 days. (4) Page Head of IR on any `stage_overdue=1`. (5) Email weekly digest of all incomplete packages.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• All upstream Art.23 UCs (UC-22.2.1, UC-22.2.7, UC-22.2.8, UC-22.2.19, UC-22.2.33) implemented and writing artefacts to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact` with fields `stage`, `parent_event_id`, and `_time`.
• Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) ≥7.0 on a Search Head or SHC.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) ≥7.7 for ITSM task correlation (optional but recommended for linking stages to ServiceNow workflow tasks).
• Splunk Add-on for Phantom (Splunkbase 3411) ≥3.0 for SOAR artefact correlation.
• Lookup `nis2_reporting_stages.csv` with columns: `profile` (standard | crossborder | dns_registry), `stage` (early_warning_24h | notification_72h | intermediate_update | final_report_1m | final_report_extension | crossborder_assessment | crossborder_task | recipient_notice | public_disclosure_assessment | csirt_guidance_ack | csirt_enisa_handoff | anonymised_data_contribution | template_completeness), `required` (yes | conditional), `deadline_hours` (numeric), `description` (human-readable).
  Example rows:
  ```
  profile,stage,required,deadline_hours,description
  standard,early_warning_24h,yes,24,24-hour early-warning to CSIRT
  standard,notification_72h,yes,72,72-hour incident notification
  standard,intermediate_update,conditional,168,Intermediate update if incident > 7 days
  standard,final_report_1m,yes,720,Final report within one month
  standard,recipient_notice,conditional,72,Notice to affected users/recipients
  standard,public_disclosure_assessment,conditional,720,Public disclosure assessment
  standard,template_completeness,yes,720,All Impl. Reg. template fields populated
  crossborder,crossborder_assessment,yes,48,Cross-border impact assessment
  crossborder,crossborder_task,yes,72,Per-country CSIRT notification task
  ```
• Lookup `nis2_significant_incident_thresholds.csv` (from UC-22.2.1) classifying ES correlation rules.
• Roles: `read` on `index=notable`, `index=audit_evidence`. Custom role `nis2_package_reader` with dashboard access.
• Network: SH must reach all indexes listed.
• License: the search reads existing artefacts (no new data generated beyond the summary-indexed findings). ~2 KB per missing-stage row × ~50 rows/run at steady state = ~100 KB/day.
• Baseline knowledge: the operator's incident profile taxonomy (which incidents are standard, which are cross-border, which are DNS/registry — per Art.28); the Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/2690 template fields.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Confirm artefact flow from upstream UCs: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact | stats dc(parent_event_id) as incidents, dc(stage) as stages, values(stage) as stage_names`. Should show multiple parent_event_ids and at least `early_warning_24h`, `notification_72h` in `stage_names`.
• If no artefacts exist, the upstream UCs haven't fired yet or their summary-indexing is misconfigured. Check each UC's alert action: `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/saved/searches | search title="UC-22.2.*" | table title action.summary_index._name action.summary_index`.
• Deploy `nis2_reporting_stages.csv`: ship via `SA-NIS2` app to the SHC deployer (`etc/shcluster/apps/SA-NIS2/lookups/`). Verify with `| inputlookup nis2_reporting_stages.csv | stats count by profile`.
• Verify the significance lookup: `| inputlookup nis2_significant_incident_thresholds.csv | where nis2_significant="yes" | stats count`. Should match the count of correlation rules the operator has classified as significant.
• Expected event volume: the search reads notables (tens to hundreds per 90 days) and artefacts (hundreds per 90 days), then produces ~1 row per missing stage. At steady state with 5–10 active significant incidents and 7 required stages each, expect ~35–70 output rows, most with `stage_overdue=0`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Use the `spl` field above. Save as scheduled search `UC-22.2.49 NIS2 Package Completeness`.

Understanding this SPL — design choices:
• **90-day window** because final reports are due at one month but may be extended (Art.23(4)(e)); the search keeps tracking until all stages are complete or the incident reaches the 90-day evidence-retention review.
• **`incident_profile` derived from `member_states_affected`** so cross-border incidents get additional required stages without manual classification. For DNS/registry entities (Art.28), add a `nis2_entity_type` lookup to classify the profile.
• **Left-join on `parent_event_id` against artefacts** so incidents with NO artefacts at all show all stages as missing.
• **`mvexpand required_stage`** explodes the lookup's per-profile stage list into one row per (incident, stage) pair.
• **`mvfind(completed_stages, required_stage)`** checks whether the stage artefact exists; `mvfind` returns ≥0 if found, null if not.
• **`stage_overdue` flag** compares `hours_open` against the stage's `deadline_hours`. This is what triggers the PagerDuty page.
• **`where stage_present = 0`** so only missing stages are surfaced. A fully complete package produces 0 rows.

Schedule and alert action:
• Cron: `0 */4 * * *` (every 4 hours). Time range `-90d to now`.
• Alert action: `Number of results where stage_overdue=1 > 0`. Throttle by `parent_event_id` for 8 hours.
• Notification: PagerDuty severity `critical` for `stage_overdue=1`; email weekly digest of all incomplete packages to Head of IR.
• Summary indexing: write findings to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search`.

Step 3 — Validate
• Inject the positive fixture: `sample-data/uc-22.2.49-fixture.json` creates an ES notable and partial artefacts. Run the SPL manually (`| savedsearch "UC-22.2.49 NIS2 Package Completeness" earliest=-90d`). Confirm the expected missing stages appear.
• Inject the negative fixture: add artefacts for all required stages. Re-run the SPL. Confirm 0 rows.
• Cross-validate with UC-22.2.33 (timeline rollup): the stages flagged as overdue here should align with the deadlines flagged in UC-22.2.33. Run both searches over the same time window and compare `parent_event_id` sets.
• Lookup validation: `| inputlookup nis2_reporting_stages.csv | where required="yes" | stats count by profile`. Standard should have ≥4 required stages; crossborder should have ≥6.
• Edge case: create an incident with `hours_open=25` (past 24h but within 72h). The search should show `early_warning_24h` as `stage_overdue=1` (deadline_hours=24) but `notification_72h` as `stage_overdue=0` (deadline_hours=72, hours_open < 72).

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard `compliance/nis2-art23-package.xml`:
• **Row 1 — Headline tiles:** Incomplete packages (count of distinct parent_event_id with ≥1 missing stage), Overdue stages (count where stage_overdue=1), Average package completeness % (across all active significant incidents).
• **Row 2 — Heatmap:** Incidents (rows, sorted by hours_open descending) × Stages (columns, ordered by deadline_hours). Cell colour: green=present, amber=approaching deadline (hours_open > deadline_hours×0.8), red=overdue, grey=not yet due. Drilldown: click a cell to open the relevant stage UC's dashboard.
• **Row 3 — Sortable table:** parent_event_id, rule_name, urgency, nis2_tier, incident_profile, owner, hours_open, required_stage, stage_description, deadline_hours, stage_overdue. Deep-link drilldown to ES Incident Review.
• **Row 4 — 90-day trend:** Stacked area chart of package completeness % per week.
• **Time picker:** Default `Last 30 days` (operations); `Last 90 days` (quarterly review).
• **Access:** `nis2_package_reader`, `head_of_ir`, `csirt_liaison`, `nis2_evidence_reader`.

Runbook (owner: Head of IR):
1. PagerDuty page on `stage_overdue=1`. Open the heatmap dashboard.
2. Identify the overdue stage. Navigate to the relevant stage UC (e.g. UC-22.2.7 for `notification_72h`).
3. Determine whether the stage is genuinely overdue or the artefact was written but to the wrong index (see KFP #6). Fix the root cause.
4. If the stage is genuinely overdue: escalate to the IR shift lead. Document the miss in the incident's audit trail. The supervisory authority may accept a late filing with documented justification.
5. Weekly digest review: group incomplete packages by `incident_profile`. Cross-border packages with >3 missing stages need CSIRT liaison involvement. Standard packages with only `template_completeness` missing need the IR analyst to fill the Implementing Regulation template fields.
6. Quarterly review: compare the 90-day trend. Declining completeness % indicates a process regression; escalate to the NIS2 programme owner.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **All incidents show all stages missing.** The upstream UCs' summary-indexing is not writing to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact`. Check the alert action on each upstream UC. Common cause: the summary index action targets `index=summary` instead of `index=audit_evidence`. Fix: update the alert action's `_name` parameter.
• **`nis2_reporting_stages.csv` returns 0 rows for `crossborder` profile.** The lookup does not have rows with `profile=crossborder`. Add the cross-border stages. The SPL will produce no cross-border stage checks until the lookup is populated.
• **`incident_profile` is always `standard` even for multi-state incidents.** The `member_states_affected` field is not populated on the notable. This field comes from UC-22.2.19's cross-border assessment artefact; if that artefact was written to `audit_evidence` but the notable itself doesn't have the field, the SPL's `eval incident_profile` will default to `standard`. Fix: enrich the notable via a lookup or join against the cross-border assessment artefact.
• **Duplicate rows for the same (incident, stage) pair.** The `nis2_reporting_stages.csv` has duplicate entries. Deduplicate with `| dedup profile stage` on the lookup.
• **`mvfind` returns false negatives.** The `stage` values in `audit_evidence` don't exactly match the lookup's `stage` column (case mismatch or whitespace). Normalize with `| eval stage = lower(trim(stage))` in both the main search and the subsearch.
• **Search takes >5 minutes.** The 90-day window over `index=notable` is too wide for large ES deployments. Narrow to 60 days or pre-filter by `urgency IN ("high","critical")` (already done in the SPL). If still slow, consider a lookup-based approach where a scheduled search pre-computes the notable-to-artefact mapping into a KV collection.

## SPL

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-90d
| lookup nis2_significant_incident_thresholds.csv rule_name OUTPUT nis2_tier nis2_significant
| where nis2_significant = "yes"
| eval hours_open = round((now() - _time) / 3600, 1)
| eval incident_profile = if(isnotnull(member_states_affected) AND mvcount(split(member_states_affected, ",")) > 0, "crossborder", "standard")
| rename event_id as parent_event_id
| join type=left parent_event_id [
    search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact earliest=-90d
    | stats values(stage) as completed_stages by parent_event_id
  ]
| eval completed_stages = coalesce(completed_stages, "none")
| lookup nis2_reporting_stages.csv profile as incident_profile OUTPUT stage as required_stage, deadline_hours, description as stage_description
| mvexpand required_stage
| eval stage_present = if(mvfind(completed_stages, required_stage) >= 0, 1, 0)
| eval stage_overdue = if(stage_present = 0 AND hours_open > deadline_hours, 1, 0)
| where stage_present = 0
| eval evidence_id = sha256(parent_event_id . required_stage . _time)
| table _time, parent_event_id, rule_name, urgency, nis2_tier, incident_profile, owner, hours_open, required_stage, stage_description, deadline_hours, stage_overdue, evidence_id
| sort - stage_overdue, - hours_open
```

## Visualization

Heatmap: incidents (rows) × stages (columns), coloured by status (green=present, amber=approaching, red=overdue, grey=not-yet-due). Single-value tiles: incomplete packages count, overdue stages count. Sortable table with drilldown to the individual stage UC.
